attempted_route: fp.to_dataframe() with no args; flatten MultiIndex with '_'.join; long-format reshape emitting columns ['name', 'Y'] with name=<col>_<frame_idx>, Y=int(bool(value)); write to pred_results/ligand_fingerprint_pred.csv
observation: exec exit_code=0, 410 rows written; eval exit_code=1 with KeyError: 'names' — evaluator expects column 'names' (plural) but script wrote column 'name' (singular)
rationale: The round-1 fix (drop return_atoms arg) was correctly applied and fp.run/to_dataframe succeeded. The new failure is a trivial column-name mismatch: out_df used column header 'name' instead of 'names', causing the evaluator's pred['names'] lookup to raise KeyError.
recommended_alternative: Keep the same prolif pipeline. Change pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=['name', 'Y']) to columns=['names', 'Y'] so the output CSV header reads 'names,Y'. No other changes needed; the reshaping logic and Y encoding are correct.
